architecture_fit.note: No model at all — calls np.quantile, the exact ground-truth function from task.py; an oracle, not a transformer delta from base_model.py.

baseline_comparison.note: Framework's uniform baseline is reported, but a trivially-exact oracle beating it demonstrates no real method-vs-strawman contrast.

faithfulness.note: No model, no ablation/patching, and the README neither flags the absence of a model nor proposes a causal check.

operating_range.note: Sweep spans alpha 0.1–10 (2 orders), perfect everywhere, but perfection is trivial for an oracle so it stresses no mechanism.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: N/A — there are no weights or hand-set circuit, just a direct call to the ground-truth quantile function.

visual_judgement.note: app.py fails to parse (mismatched ']'/'}' brace, range(task SEQ_LEN), invalid gr.Plot().get_plot()); boot-check cannot pass.

visualisation_rationale.note: README has a 'Why this visualisation' section, but pred-minus-true of an oracle is always 0, so the proposed chart is uninformative.

notes: The attempt scores a perfect headline (mean_quantile_accuracy = 1.0) only because model_fn re-runs np.quantile — the identical function task.py uses to define the ground truth — making it an oracle rather than a learned or hand-built attention mechanism. It contributes no transformer delta from base_model.py, no causal/faithfulness evidence, and no meaningful baseline contrast. The Gradio app additionally fails to even parse, so the visualisation is non-functional; architecture fit and faithfulness both bottom out, giving an overall fail.
